assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and internet access.
No platform account, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Create an Algolia account and use REST calls to add one object and retrieve it in search results.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated REST write stores an object and a search request returns that object as a hit.
first_success.observable_signal: The save-object response succeeds and the subsequent search JSON contains the object in hits.

route_selection.surface: Search REST API via cURL: save an object, then search
route_selection.rule: Prefer a hosted or direct API route over local installation, and prefer documented cURL when it reaches the same first success.
route_selection.selected: Search REST API via cURL: save an object, then search

candidates.name: Search REST API via cURL: save an object, then search
candidates.reason: The official REST API reaches indexed and searchable data with cURL and no local SDK or frontend project.

candidates.name: React InstantSearch quickstart
candidates.reason: It adds Node.js, packages, source files, and a local UI after the core write-and-search API boundary.

required_path.action: Create an Algolia account and open an application in the dashboard.
Copy the application ID and the API keys needed to write and search.
required_path.observable_result: Credentials are available for the REST headers.

required_path.action: Use cURL to call the Save object endpoint for a test index and object ID.
Wait for the indexing task to complete when the response identifies an asynchronous task.
Use cURL to search the test index for the saved object.
required_path.observable_result: The search response includes the saved object in hits.

prerequisites.description: The application ID, a write-capable API key, and a search-capable API key.

external_gates.description: REST requests require the application ID and API key headers.

unavoidable_waits.description: Indexing can be asynchronous before the object appears in search.

platform_outcomes.description: An authenticated REST write stores an object and a search request returns that object as a hit.

excluded.reason: These occur after the first observable end-to-end result.

uncertainties.question: The authenticated dashboard may create an initial application automatically or require the developer to create one.
uncertainties.impact: The required path may differ for some fresh accounts.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account walkthrough through the authenticated first-success state.
